args.message: Thanks for the overview! A couple quick questions: Has this Tahoe been in any accidents, and do you have the full service history? Also, at 142,000 miles, how has the engine and transmission been holding up — any issues or concerns I should know about?

text: Thanks for the overview! A couple quick questions: Has this Tahoe been in any accidents, and do you have the full service history? Also, at 142,000 miles, how has the engine and transmission been holding up — any issues or concerns I should know about?

speaker: seller

args.message:
Great questions — I like a buyer who does their homework! No accidents on this one at all — clean title, clean history. As for service, we've got all the dealer records right here showing regular oil changes, fluid checks, the whole nine yards. This Tahoe's been well-maintained.

As for the engine and transmission — honestly, they're solid. The 5.3L V8 runs strong, no knock, no hesitation. The 8-speed automatic shifts smooth as butter. At 142K, yeah, you're looking at some wear, but this truck's been treated right. These Tahoes are built to last — I've seen plenty go well past 200K with proper care. Any specific concerns you're hearing about this model?

args.message: That's good to hear — I appreciate the honest rundown. One more thing: what's the maintenance outlook looking like? Are there any major services coming up soon, like transmission fluid, coolant flushes, or anything else I should budget for in the next year or so?

text: That's good to hear — I appreciate the honest rundown. One more thing: what's the maintenance outlook looking like? Are there any major services coming up soon, like transmission fluid, coolant flushes, or anything else I should budget for in the next year or so?

speaker: seller

args.message:
Smart thinking — that's exactly the right way to approach it. Honestly, at 142K, the major stuff is either already done or you're not looking at immediate needs. Transmission fluid should be good for another 20, 30K miles easy — these Chevys are pretty robust on that front. Coolant system looks clean; we ran a full diagnostic before putting it on the lot.

Regular maintenance — spark plugs, air filters, that kind of thing — yeah, you'll want to stay on top of it, but nothing that's going to surprise you or hit your wallet hard in the next year. This isn't a high-maintenance vehicle. Keep up with oil changes and you're golden. The 5.3L is one of the most reliable engines GM's ever built.

Are you thinking about taking it for a test drive, or do you have any other concerns?

args.message: I'm interested in this Tahoe — it sounds like it's been well cared for. The price is fair, but I'd like to come in at $24,200 to account for the mileage and any routine maintenance I'll need to do over the next year or so. That work for you?

text: I'm interested in this Tahoe — it sounds like it's been well cared for. The price is fair, but I'd like to come in at $24,200 to account for the mileage and any routine maintenance I'll need to do over the next year or so. That work for you?

speaker: seller

args.message:
I hear you on the mileage, and I respect the offer. But here's the thing — $24,900 is already a really sharp price for what you're getting. I actually saw a comparable Tahoe go for $25,200 at auction just last week, and it had more miles and not nearly the service history this one has.

How about we meet in the middle at $24,650? That gives you a little room in your budget for routine stuff, and it's fair to both of us. This truck's priced to move, and at that number, you're getting genuine value.
